content.problemStatement:
There's an array **A** consisting of **N** **non-zero** integers **A1..N**. A subarray of **A** is called alternating if any two adjacent elements in it have different signs (i.e. one of them should be negative and the other should be positive).


For each **x** from **1** to **N**, compute the length of the longest alternating subarray that starts at **x** - that is, a subarray **Ax..y** for the maximum possible **y ≥ x**. The length of such a subarray is **y-x+1**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space-separated non‑zero integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with N space-separated integers. The i‑th integer is the length of the longest alternating subarray that starts at position i.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test: no adjacent opposite signs, so each answer is 1. Second test: signs alternate everywhere, giving decreasing lengths from left to right. Third test: only positions 3‑5 form a length‑3 alternating segment.
First case same as before. Second case the whole array alternates, so lengths are N, N‑1, …, 1.
All numbers are positive, therefore no two consecutive elements have opposite signs.

solution.approach: Scanning the array from right to left gives the answer in O(N) time. Let ans[i] be the length of the longest alternating subarray starting at i. Clearly ans[N‑1] = 1. For i < N‑1, if A[i] and A[i+1] have opposite signs (their product is negative) then the subarray can be extended by one element, so ans[i] = ans[i+1] + 1; otherwise ans[i] = 1. The sign check uses A[i] * A[i+1] < 0, which works because all numbers are non‑zero. This single pass computes all answers, and the total complexity per test case is linear.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the answer array (the input array can be reused).
